title: T-001 — Factory release runtime preflight guard (08-PROD-a, §1.1A / FR-08-E1-001A)
prompt:
# T-001 — Factory release runtime preflight guard

PRD: docs/prd/08-PRODUCTION-PRD.md §1.1A, §1.2, §7.1 FR-08-E1-001A, §7.2 FR-08-E2-001
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement the Production runtime preflight guard that admits a WO into START / material consumption / output registration only when the canonical factory release read model (owned by 01-NPD T-097, adapted by 03-TECHNICAL T-080/T-081) reports `approved_for_factory` or `released_to_factory` for the WO snapshot's `active_bom_header_id` + `active_factory_spec_id`. All other states return typed `release_blockers` without mutating runtime state.

## Implementation contract
1. apps/web/lib/production/factory-release-runtime-guards.ts exports `assertFactoryReleaseRuntimeAdmitted(wo)` returning either `{ admitted: true }` or `{ admitted: false, blockers: ReleaseBlocker[] }`.
2. Reads the canonical read model via the 03-TECHNICAL adapter (T-080/T-081); never queries `latest` BOM/spec, D365 sync flags, or local `bom_released`.
3. Maps canonical statuses: `approved_for_factory` | `released_to_factory` → admit; `pending_npd_release` | `pending_technical_approval` | `blocked` | missing `active_bom_header_id` | missing `active_factory_spec_id` | snapshot mismatch (active IDs differ from canonical row) → blocker.
4. D365 push success/failure must never change canonical release state nor admit a WO.
5. Wired into start-wo, consume-to-WO, output registration services as a single-line guard; UI surfaces (dashboard, WO list, WO detail badge) read the same blockers via a thin service.

## Files
- apps/web/lib/production/factory-release-runtime-guards.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/production/dashboard-service.ts [modify]
- packages/api-services/production/start-wo.ts [modify]
- packages/api-services/production/consume-to-wo.ts [modify]
- packages/api-services/production/register-output.ts [modify]
- tests/lib/production/factory-release-runtime-guards.test.ts [create]
- tests/actions/production/work-order-start-release-preflight.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a WO whose snapshot `active_bom_header_id` and `active_factory_spec_id` match the canonical row and status is `approved_for_factory` or `released_to_factory`, when START is invoked, then preflight returns `admitted: true` and runtime state mutates normally.
2. Given canonical status is `pending_npd_release`, `pending_technical_approval`, `blocked`, or any required active ID is missing/mismatched, when any of START / consume-to-WO / output registration is invoked, then service returns typed `release_blockers` and no runtime mutation occurs.
3. Given a WO has D365 Built/export/synced metadata or local `bom_released=true` but the canonical row is not factory-usable, when admit is requested, then result is blocked and `release_blockers` includes the canonical reason (D365 metadata is not consulted).

## Test strategy
- RED: write tests/lib/production/factory-release-runtime-guards.test.ts + tests/actions/production/work-order-start-release-preflight.test.ts asserting the status matrix, missing/mismatched active IDs, and D365/local flag no-op before the guard exists.
- Run `pnpm vitest run tests/lib/production/factory-release-runtime-guards.test.ts tests/actions/production/work-order-start-release-preflight.test.ts`
- Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not revive or implement Production ReleaseWoModal / DRAFT→READY release flow (deprecated per `prototype-index-production.json`).
- Do not create a Production-owned release enum/model.
- Do not infer factory availability from D365 Built/export/sync or local `bom_released` flags.
- Do not select a newer/latest BOM/spec at START time.

pipeline_inputs.description: Production runtime preflight + typed release_blockers for canonical factory release read model; admits only approved/released active_bom_header_id + active_factory_spec_id, ignores D365 sync.
pipeline_inputs.details: Single service `assertFactoryReleaseRuntimeAdmitted` consumed by start-wo, consume-to-WO, output registration services and dashboard/list/detail badge service. Canonical read model is owned by 01-NPD T-097 and adapted by 03-TECHNICAL T-080/T-081; if the canonical adapter is not yet present, fail loudly rather than emitting fallback logic. Snapshot mismatch (WO active IDs differ from canonical active row) blocks with `release_blockers=[snapshot_mismatch]` rather than auto-selecting newer BOM/spec. D365 confirmation push is an asynchronous side-effect after local production events and must never modify canonical release state.
out_of_scope: Do not implement the canonical release read model itself (01-NPD T-097)
Do not implement the Technical approval/transition adapter (03-TECHNICAL T-080/T-081)
Do not build UI surfaces (T-046 wires blocker badges into dashboard/list/detail)
Do not implement Planning WO snapshot capture (04-PLANNING-BASIC T-001)
acceptance_criteria: Given a WO whose snapshot active_bom_header_id+active_factory_spec_id match the canonical row and status is approved_for_factory or released_to_factory, when START is invoked, then preflight returns admitted:true and runtime state mutates normally.

Given canonical status is pending_npd_release/pending_technical_approval/blocked or any required active ID is missing/mismatched, when START/consume-to-WO/output registration is invoked, then service returns typed release_blockers and no runtime mutation occurs.

Given a WO has D365 Built/export/synced metadata or local bom_released=true but the canonical row is not factory-usable, when admit is requested, then result is blocked and D365 metadata is not consulted as approval evidence.

Given an LP/lot has an active quality hold (v_active_holds), when WO consume is attempted, then the operation is rejected with 409 quality_hold_active and an outbox event production.consume.blocked is emitted (consume gate per 09-quality T-064).
test_strategy: RED: write tests/lib/production/factory-release-runtime-guards.test.ts + tests/actions/production/work-order-start-release-preflight.test.ts covering status matrix, missing/mismatched active IDs and D365/local flag no-op before the guard exists.
risk_red_lines: Do not revive or implement Production ReleaseWoModal / DRAFT→READY release flow (deprecated label).
Do not create a Production-owned release enum/model.
Do not infer factory availability from D365 Built/export/sync or local bom_released flags.
Do not select a newer/latest BOM/spec at START time.
Do not bypass the 09-quality consume gate (T-064 v_active_holds) — every consume path must call holdsGuard(lpId, lotId) before mutating consumption tables.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Canonical factory release status/read model owner.
Technical adapter exposes factory-use guards and badge/blocker mapping.
Planning snapshots active_bom_header_id and active_factory_spec_id onto WOs before Production runtime.

Quality consume gate (v_active_holds) — every LP/WO consume operation must call holdsGuard(lpId, lotId) before mutating consumption/output state. Active hold returns 409 quality_hold_active and emits production.consume.blocked outbox event (PRD §16.4 V-PROD-02/V-PROD-16).
